title: Error writing blob: Error saving image to docker engine: Error response from daemon: client version 1.22 is too old. Minimum supported API version is 1.24, please upgrade your client to a newer version
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

I am trying to use buildah to push to a docker daemon running in docker desktop 4.28.0 but I get the following error

`Error writing blob: Error saving image to docker engine: Error response from daemon: client version 1.22 is too old. Minimum supported API version is 1.24, please upgrade your client to a newer version`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build container using buildah
2. Push to docker using this command `buildah push my-container docker-daemon:my-container:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**
`Error writing blob: Error saving image to docker engine: Error response from daemon: client version 1.22 is too old. Minimum supported API version is 1.24, please upgrade your client to a newer version`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Push should succeed

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/oldstable,now 1.19.6+dfsg1-1+b6 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.19.6
Go Version:      go1.15.9
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.10.3
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```
**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
# apt list buildah
Listing... Done
buildah/oldstable,now 1.19.6+dfsg1-1+b6 amd64 [installed]
# ^C
# buildah version
Version:         1.19.6
Go Version:      go1.15.9
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.10.3
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
# ^C
# cat /etc/release
cat: /etc/release: No such file or directory
# bash
root@0fc2e30f4ce7:/app# cat /etc/release
cat: /etc/release: No such file or directory
root@0fc2e30f4ce7:/app# cat /etc/*release~
cat: '/etc/*release~': No such file or directory
root@0fc2e30f4ce7:/app# cat /etc/*release
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"

BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/"
root@0fc2e30f4ce7:/app# ^C
root@0fc2e30f4ce7:/app# 
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux 0fc2e30f4ce7 5.15.146.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Thu Jan 11 04:09:03 UTC 2024 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

File does not exist.

** docker version output **

```
Client:
 Cloud integration: v1.0.35+desktop.11
 Version:           25.0.3
 API version:       1.44

  GitCommit:        de40ad0
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Any updated on when docker client version 1.24 will be supported on buildah? Thanks.

YOu are using an ancient version of buildah

```
$ buildah -v
buildah version 1.35.3 (image-spec 1.1.0, runtime-spec 1.1.0)
$ buildah version
Version:         1.35.3
Go Version:      go1.22.1
Image Spec:      1.1.0
Runtime Spec:    1.1.0
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.30.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Mon Apr  1 07:04:32 2024
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

@rhatdan Thanks for pointing that out. It looks like the most up to date version from apt-get in Debian 11. Is there a way to get the docker api version supported for a particular version of buildah? Thanks.
Sorry we do not support older versions in the upstream. It is up to distributions like RHEL and Debian to support older versions.

@rhatdan I understand. I am not asking for any change to be made. What I am looking for is a way to determine what docker api version is supported for a particular version so I can determine what base container to use for my application that will have the supported buildah package. Is there a specific place in the code this is documented or possible some command I can run to get that information? Thanks.
@rhatdan @baude @mheon Any update on a way to determine what docker api version is supported in a specific version of buildah? Thanks.
This seems like more of a @mtrmac question given `docker-daemon` is a c/image transport?
We don't keep track of what versions of Buildah supported which docker-daemon version.

c/image has stopped using version 1.22 in c/image 5.29.2 , but that’s not directly helpful in the way you are probably looking for.

Since c/image 5.29.3, the supported versions are determined by the included version of the Docker client, a dependency that is being updated fairly often but not with a guaranteed timeline. It can be determined by inspecting the `vendor` subtree of any particular Buildah release, but as Dan says, we don’t keep a table.
